ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'mushroom soup' class (FOODON_03311737); no specific Enoki/Golden Needle Mushroom Soup entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mushroom-based soup — composite dish, close analogous match to enoki mushroom soup
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to golden needle mushroom soup exists. A00LC (mushroom-based soup composite) is the closest defensible analogous code, treated as a closest_parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific dish. Not required for food-only records.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Golden Needle Mushroom Soup is one bowl of approximately 250 g, typically served as a side dish or starter alongside a larger Chinese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Mushrooms, enoki, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #11260
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central enoki mushroom (raw) data combined with a dilution factor reflecting a clear broth preparation (approximately 30–35% mushroom solids in a water-based broth). Input AI-generated values were cross-checked against USDA data and retained where consistent. Energy 18 kcal/100 g, protein 1.2 g, fat 0.5 g, carbohydrate 2.8 g are plausible for a dilute enoki mushroom soup. Sodium 160 mg/100 g reflects added broth seasoning. All values documented as adapted estimates.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mushroom-based foods — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for golden needle mushroom soup. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on the very low carbohydrate content (2.8 g/100 g), high dietary fibre (1.5 g/100 g), and the established low-GI profile of mushroom-based broths and non-starchy vegetable soups documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (15 × 7.0 g) / 100 = 1.05. Carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 2.8 × 2.5 = 7.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (160 mg/100 g) from broth; high potassium (180 mg/100 g) supports blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Golden Needle Mushroom Soup is generally suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its high potassium-to-sodium ratio; however, patients should request low-sodium broth or home-prepare the soup to keep sodium below 400 mg per serving, as commercial broths can significantly increase sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.8 g/100 g), high dietary fibre (1.5 g/100 g), and low glycemic index (~15) minimise postprandial glucose excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Golden Needle Mushroom Soup is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its low glycemic index of approximately 15 and glycemic load of approximately 1 per 250 g serving make it an excellent low-impact side dish that supports blood glucose management without requiring portion restriction.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (0.5 g/100 g), negligible saturated fat, and bioactive polysaccharides in enoki mushrooms associated with cardiovascular-protective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Golden Needle Mushroom Soup is appropriate for individuals with cardiovascular disease given its very low total fat and saturated fat content; enoki mushrooms contain beta-glucans and flammulin, bioactive compounds that may support immune modulation and lipid metabolism, though patients on sodium-restricted diets should monitor broth sodium levels.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant polysaccharides and anti-inflammatory compounds in enoki mushrooms
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Golden Needle Mushroom Soup may be beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties of enoki mushroom polysaccharides; it is a clean, low-purine-adjacent dish, though those on strict low-purine diets should note that mushrooms contain moderate purine levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Needle Mushroom Soup (金针菇汤, Jīn Zhēn Gū Tāng) is a light, delicate Chinese soup built around enoki mushrooms (Flammulina velutipes), which are characterised by their long, slender white stems, small caps, mild flavour, and pleasantly crunchy texture. The mushrooms are simmered in a clear vegetable or chicken broth, often with rehydrated goji berries that add a subtle sweetness and visual colour contrast. The soup is commonly garnished with chopped spring onions and a few drops of sesame oil to enhance its clean, savoury aroma. Nutritionally, it is extremely low in calories (approximately 18 kcal per 100 g) and carbohydrates (2.8 g per 100 g), with a low glycemic index of approximately 15, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It provides meaningful dietary fibre (1.5 g per 100 g), potassium (180 mg), and B-vitamins including niacin (2.0 mg) and riboflavin (0.08 mg). In Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), the soup is valued for its 'cooling' properties and is considered a restorative, digestive-friendly dish. It is served as a side dish or starter at lunch and dinner and is suitable for vegetarian and vegan diets. The soup is widely consumed across mainland China and in Chinese communities throughout Southeast Asia, including Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central enoki mushroom (raw) data adjusted for a dilute broth preparation. No exact approved-source entry for golden needle mushroom soup as a composite dish was found. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source provides a direct composite-dish nutrient profile for this preparation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy enoki mushroom (raw) data (FDC #169403) with dilution adjustment for a clear broth preparation. Input AI-generated values were cross-checked and retained where consistent with USDA data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this dilute mushroom soup preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite soup preparation is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central for the prepared dish. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: enoki mushrooms are a plant-based food and naturally contain no vitamin B12; vitamin D content in commercially grown enoki mushrooms (grown without UV exposure) is negligible and confirmed near-zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based mushroom soup with no animal-derived fat components (sesame oil garnish is optional and plant-based). 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero in this minimally processed plant-based soup. 7. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for golden needle mushroom soup was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Portions in input contained a duplicate entry; deduplicated to a single 250 g serving. 9. Sodium of 160 mg/100 g reflects a lightly seasoned broth; commercial preparations may be significantly higher. 10. The input source (Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry) was not used as the primary nutrient source as it is not on the approved source list for nutrient values; USDA FoodData Central was used instead. The journal reference is retained as supporting context for health guidance claims regarding enoki mushroom bioactive compounds.
